mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and construction investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels during a monetary tightening cycle trigger aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes fail to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, with directional accuracy below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, which directly translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment surge exceeding 200 thousand signals robust economic momentum and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200 thousand
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of months following a PAYEMS increase above 200 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth across production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio for 60-day forward returns, indicating no predictive edge.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during a late-cycle expansion signals an impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with SPX posting positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered cases.
